name: Jacom
description_short: Japanese American Community Credit Union offers member-owned financial services including deposits, loans, mortgages, and access to 30,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide.
description_long:
JACom CU (Japanese American Community Credit Union) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving the Japanese American community and beyond. The organization emphasizes its mission with the motto 'Once a Member, Always a Member,' reflecting credit union principles of member loyalty and service. The credit union has expanded its reach through partnerships and shared networks to provide comprehensive financial services.

JACom CU offers a full range of deposit and loan products including savings accounts, checking accounts, student loans, personal loans, and real estate financing. Members have access to online banking, mobile banking, Zelle money transfer services, and a loan application portal. The credit union provides mortgage services through a partnership with Right Choice Mortgage, offering conventional loans, government-backed programs (VA and FHA), and specialized mortgages for borrowers with previous financial challenges. First trust deed products include 30-, 20-, 15-, and 10-year fixed mortgages plus adjustable-rate options.

JACom CU distinguishes itself through participation in national networks that extend member convenience far beyond a single branch. Members can access over 5,000 shared branch locations nationwide and over 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs through the CO-OP Network, including locations at Costco and 7-Eleven. The credit union also offers partnerships with Autoland for vehicle purchases and provides rate and loan information through online calculators and newsletters. Mobile app access allows members to manage accounts remotely.

As a credit union, JACom CU operates under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) oversight and follows not-for-profit principles. However, the website provides limited specific information about deposit rates, loan rates, membership eligibility criteria, or current promotional offers. Prospective members will need to contact the credit union directly or visit in person for detailed rate information and loan application specifics.
services: Share savings accounts and checking accounts
Mortgage lending (conventional, VA, FHA, reverse mortgages, post-bankruptcy programs)
Online banking platform with sign-in and enrollment
Mobile app for remote account access
Loan application portal and rate information
CO-OP ATM Network access (30,000+ surcharge-free locations)
Right Choice Mortgage partnership for real estate services
pros: Access to 30,000+ surcharge-free ATMs nationwide through CO-OP Network
Over 5,000 shared branch locations nationwide for member convenience
Partnership with Right Choice Mortgage for expanded real estate services
Full suite of loan products including government-backed VA and FHA mortgages
Mobile app available for remote account management
Zelle money transfer integration for peer-to-peer payments
Autoland partnership simplifies vehicle purchasing for members
cons: Website does not display current deposit or loan rates, requiring members to inquire separately
Limited information about membership eligibility requirements or application process
No specific details provided about fee structures or account minimums
Community focus on Japanese American heritage may create perception of limited accessibility
Mortgage services rely on external partner (Right Choice Mortgage) rather than in-house origination
best_for: Japanese American community members seeking culturally-focused financial services
Members who value nationwide ATM and branch access through shared networks
Borrowers seeking mortgage options including VA, FHA, and specialized programs
Credit union members seeking lower-cost financial services with personal service
diagnosis: JACom CU is best for members seeking full-service credit union banking with exceptional nationwide ATM and branch access, particularly those in the Japanese American community. The main caveat is that prospective members must contact the credit union directly to obtain current rates, eligibility details, and specific loan terms, as this information is not published on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Jacom — NCUA-insured credit union in los angeles, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
